{{Short description|5th century Kofun in Awaiwa, Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Ryōgūzan Kofun | native_name =両宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ryoguzan Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Akaiwa, Okayama|Akaiwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in the [[Bizen Province|Bizen region]] and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding [[paddy field]]s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of [[Emperor Nintoku]] in [[Sakai, Osaka]]), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Seto Station]]. The [[Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|5th century Kofun in Awaiwa, Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Ryōgūzan Kofun | native_name =両宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ryoguzan Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Akaiwa, Okayama|Akaiwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in the [[Bizen Province|Bizen region]] and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding [[paddy field]]s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of [[Emperor Nintoku]] in [[Sakai, Osaka]]), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Seto Station]]. The [[Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|Panoramic view Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun </gallery>

{{Short description|5th century Kofun in Awaiwa, Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Ryōgūzan Kofun | native_name =両宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ryoguzan Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Akaiwa, Okayama|Akaiwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in the [[Bizen Province|Bizen region]] and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding [[paddy field]]s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of [[Emperor Nintoku]] in [[Sakai, Osaka]]), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Seto Station]]. The [[Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Seto Station]]. The [[Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|5th century Kofun in Awaiwa, Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Ryōgūzan Kofun | native_name =両宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ryoguzan Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Akaiwa, Okayama|Akaiwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in the [[Bizen Province|Bizen region]] and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding [[paddy field]]s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of [[Emperor Nintoku]] in [[Sakai, Osaka]]), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Seto Station]]. The [[Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|Panoramic view Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun </gallery>

{{Short description|5th century Kofun in Awaiwa, Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Ryōgūzan Kofun | native_name =両宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ryoguzan Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Akaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in the [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen region]] and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Seto Station|Seto Station]]. The [[:en:Bizen Kokubun-ji|Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|Panoramic view Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Ryoguzan Kofun}} *[https://www.city.akaiwa.lg.jp/bunkazai/ichiran/cyuumoku/ryouguzan/2279.html Akaiwa City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kurashikikoukokan.com/yomoyama/2015/202.html Kurashiki Arcaehological Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Completion tier 3]]

This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Seto Station|Seto Station]]. The [[:en:Bizen Kokubun-ji|Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11362631]] Wikidata name: Ryoguzan Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|5th century Kofun in Awaiwa, Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Ryōgūzan Kofun | native_name =両宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ryoguzan Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.739778|N|134.0026|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Akaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in the [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen region]] and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Seto Station|Seto Station]]. The [[:en:Bizen Kokubun-ji|Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ryoguzan Kofun]][[en:Ryōgūzan Kofun]][[ja:両宮山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11362631]] Wikidata name: Ryoguzan Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|5th century Kofun in Awaiwa, Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Ryōgūzan Kofun | native_name =両宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ryoguzan Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.739778|N|134.0026|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Akaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in the [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen region]] and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Seto Station|Seto Station]]. The [[:en:Bizen Kokubun-ji|Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|Panoramic view Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in the [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen region]] and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Seto Station|Seto Station]]. The [[:en:Bizen Kokubun-ji|Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|Panoramic view Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.

The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Seto Station|Seto Station]]. The [[:en:Bizen Kokubun-ji|Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ryoguzan Kofun]][[en:Ryōgūzan Kofun]][[ja:両宮山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11362631]] Wikidata name: Ryoguzan Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|5th century Kofun in Awaiwa, Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Ryōgūzan Kofun | native_name =両宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ryoguzan Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.739778|N|134.0026|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Akaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in the [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen region]] and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Seto Station|Seto Station]]. The [[:en:Bizen Kokubun-ji|Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|Panoramic view Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ryoguzan Kofun]][[en:Ryōgūzan Kofun]][[ja:両宮山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11362631]] Wikidata name: Ryoguzan Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|5th century Kofun in Awaiwa, Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Ryōgūzan Kofun | native_name =両宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ryoguzan Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.739778|N|134.0026|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Akaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in the [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen region]] and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Seto Station|Seto Station]]. The [[:en:Bizen Kokubun-ji|Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in the [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen region]] and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Seto Station|Seto Station]]. The [[:en:Bizen Kokubun-ji|Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|Panoramic view Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun </gallery>

The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Seto Station|Seto Station]]. The [[:en:Bizen Kokubun-ji|Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Ryoguzan Kofun]][[en:Ryōgūzan Kofun]][[ja:両宮山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Ryoguzan Kofun]][[en:Ryōgūzan Kofun]][[ja:両宮山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ryoguzan Kofun]][[en:Ryōgūzan Kofun]][[ja:両宮山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11362631]] Wikidata name: Ryoguzan Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|5th century Kofun in Awaiwa, Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Ryōgūzan Kofun | native_name =両宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ryoguzan Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.739778|N|134.0026|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Akaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in the [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen region]] and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Seto Station|Seto Station]]. The [[:en:Bizen Kokubun-ji|Bizen Kokubun-ji]] ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|5th century Kofun in Awaiwa, Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ryōgūzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.739778|N|134.0026|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Akaiwa]], {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}} in the {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen region}} and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}}s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} in {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Seto Station|en|Seto Station|WD=Q844625|lt=Seto Station}}. The {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|en|Bizen Kokubun-ji|WD=Q11387321|lt=Bizen Kokubun-ji}} ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|Panoramic view Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Ryoguzan Kofun}} *[https://www.city.akaiwa.lg.jp/bunkazai/ichiran/cyuumoku/ryouguzan/2279.html Akaiwa City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kurashikikoukokan.com/yomoyama/2015/202.html Kurashiki Arcaehological Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|23.2|N|134|00|9.36|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ryōgūzan Kofun'''|両宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of [[:en:Akaiwa, Okayama|Awaiwa]], {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924 with the area under protection expanded in 1978 and again in 2006.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}} in the {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen region}} and the third largest in Okayama Prefecture, and the 39th largest in Japan.
==Overview== The Ryōgūzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is part of the Ryōgūzan Kofun cluster (also known as the Nishi-Takatsuki Kofun cluster) which consists of four keyhole-shaped tombs and two scallop-shaped tombs, including this one.
Built in the latter half of the 5th century, or the middle of the Kofun period, the Ryōgūzan Kofun has a surrounding moat and a total length of 206 meters. An [[archaeological excavation]] from 2002 to 2004 discovered a new moat around the outside of the surrounding moat, revealing that the original construction had a double moat. Including the two surrounding moats, the total length of the Kofun reaches 349 meters on the main axis. The outer moat is buried under surrounding {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}}s, but the inner moat is still filled with water (and is used as a reservoir for agriculture). The tumulus itself is about 120 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion, 100 meter in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and about 20 meters high at both portions. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. Since the tumulus itself has not been excavated, the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are is unknown. The dimensions of the tumulus appear to be based on a two-fifths scale of the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisenryō Kofun]] (tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} in {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}), and indicates that the person buried at the Ryōgūzan Kofun was an influential person with strong ties to theYamato kingdom.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
To the north of the Ryōgūzan Kofun is a ''baizuka'' ancillary Kofun, the {{nihongo|'''Wada Chausuyama Kofun'''|和田茶臼山古墳|}}, with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of about 5 meters. This smaller tumulus also originally had a double moat, although it appears that part of its outer moat was shared with that of the Ryōgūzan Kofun. Currently, only the round rear part of the mound remains, but excavations confirmed the front part once existed. As with the Ryōgūzan Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' have been found. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 5th century.
The tumulus is about 10 minutes by car from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Seto Station|en|Seto Station|WD=Q844625|lt=Seto Station}}. The {{ill|Bizen Kokubun-ji|en|Bizen Kokubun-ji|WD=Q11387321|lt=Bizen Kokubun-ji}} ruins are located on the west side of Ryōgūzan Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ryoguzan Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|Panoramic view Wada Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Wada Chausuyama Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
